text: JWT is the most widely used token format for web API authentication, replacing server-side sessions in stateless architectures

JWT (JSON Web Token) is a compact, URL-safe token format standardized as IETF RFC 7519. It enables stateless authentication by encoding claims (user identity, permissions, expiry) in a self-contained token signed by the server. JWT is the most widely used token format for web API authentication, replacing server-side sessions in stateless architect
